title: daviesia benthamii subsp. acanthoclona
intro: Daviesia benthamii subsp. acanthoclona is a sub-species of plant within the Daviesia genus, commonly known as 'pea-flower bush', which is native to the eastern part of Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various gastrointestinal issues. The plant is believed to possess health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing symptoms of conditions like diarrhea and dysentery. Daviesia benthamii subsp. acanthoclona can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas and infusions, which are often used by indigenous Australians for treating various ailments.
